title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Automation and Data Client Syst… · Program Brief: Safe Idempotent Methods
summary: A grant-report automation creates export jobs where interrupted POST requests and duplicate submissions can produce conflicting artifacts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py safe idempotent methods increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish safe and idempotent method semantics from what one particular server happens to do. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
